 {"id":"brenner_bot-0kp","title":"Create first-time user onboarding flow","description":"# Task

Design and implement an onboarding experience for first-time visitors.

# The Problem

Users arriving at brennerbot.org need to quickly understand:
1. What is this? (A research tool based on Sydney Brenner's method)
2. Who was Brenner? (Brief bio)
3. Why does this matter? (Value proposition)
4. What can I do here? (Key actions)

# Onboarding Elements

## 1. Hero Section (already planned)
- Animated quotes give a taste of Brenner's voice
- Clear value proposition

## 2. 'What Is This?' Section (NEW)
A brief, scannable explanation:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  WHAT IS BRENNER BOT?                                        │
│                                                              │
│  Sydney Brenner (1927-2019) was one of the most successful  │
│  experimental biologists in history. But his METHOD is more  │
│  valuable than any single discovery.                         │
│                                                              │
│  This project reverse-engineers his cognitive architecture   │
│  and makes it executable for AI-assisted research.           │
│                                                              │
│  [📖 Read the Corpus]  [🔬 Explore the Method]               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 3. Quick Tour Option
- 'Take a quick tour' button
- Step-through overlay highlighting key sections
- Can be dismissed / 'Don't show again'

## 4. Guided Entry Points
Instead of just 'Browse Corpus', offer guided paths:
- 'New to Brenner? Start here →' (curated intro excerpts)
- 'Compare AI syntheses →' (distillations comparison)
- 'Ready to research? Start a session →'

# Implementation

## Tour Library
Use something like `react-joyride` or `intro.js` for the guided tour.

## Persistence
Store 'tour completed' in localStorage to not show again.

## Mobile
Simpler onboarding on mobile - maybe just the 'What Is This?' section without overlay tour.

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 'What Is This?' section explains the project
- [ ] Optional tour available for new users
- [ ] Guided entry points help users choose their path
- [ ] Tour state persists (don't show again)
- [ ] Works on mobile","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:22:34.53227297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:27:49.046913869-05:00","labels":["onboarding","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0kp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-9o2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.972337943-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0kp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-hv6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:07.002380154-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0kp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ggv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:27:49.010916091-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-0li","title":"Create distillations overview page with model cards","description":"# Task

Build the /distillations overview page that introduces all three model syntheses.

# Page Structure

1. **Header** - 'The Three Distillations' with brief intro
2. **Model Cards** - One card per model (Opus, GPT, Gemini)
3. **Comparison CTA** - Link to comparison view

# Model Card Content

Each card shows:
- Model name + logo/icon
- Signature color (Opus: orange, GPT: blue, Gemini: cyan)
- Metaphor ('Philosophy of Science' / 'Decision Theory' / 'Operating System')
- Core question it answers
- 2-3 unique contributions
- 'Read Full' button

# Layout

- 3-column grid on desktop
- Single column on mobile
- Cards have equal height

# Why This First

Users need an entry point to understand the distillations before diving into comparison. This page provides that orientation.

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Three model cards render correctly
- [ ] Each card has correct content
- [ ] Links to individual distillation pages work
- [ ] Comparison CTA visible
- [ ] Responsive layout","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:20:22.627743856-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:20.05337566-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:20.05337566-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: /distillations page has model cards, comparison table, convergences section, reading guide, responsive layout","labels":["distillations","overview","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:00.256561797-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-igw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:25:02.93339356-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:25:28.455279492-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:25:28.508908397-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0rs","title":"Docs: fix apps/web test run commands","description":"## Problem
Several vitest-based unit tests in `apps/web/src/lib/*.test.ts` have header comments suggesting running them with `bun test ...`.

## Why this matters
AGENTS.md is explicit: for the web app, tests must be run via `cd apps/web && bun run test` so Vitest + happy-dom are configured. Suggesting `bun test` causes confusing failures (e.g. "document is not defined") and makes local dev slower.

## Fix
Update the header comments to recommend:
- `cd apps/web && bun run test -- <file>`

## Acceptance
- No behavior changes; only comments.
- Grep for `Run with: bun test apps/web` returns nothing.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T06:30:51.92657549-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T06:49:08.996370501-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T06:49:08.996370501-05:00","close_reason":"Updated vitest header comments to use: cd apps/web && bun run test -- <file>","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0rs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.7.4.1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T06:30:51.94956319-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-0s7","title":"Legacy: Build excerpt builder component (superseded)","description":"# Task

Create the excerpt builder that allows users to select text from documents and build cited excerpt blocks.

# User Flow

1. User browses a document (e.g., transcript)
2. User selects text
3. Selection popover appears: 'Add to excerpt'
4. Selection added to 'Excerpt Basket' (sidebar or drawer)
5. User can add multiple selections
6. User composes final excerpt with ordering and context
7. Export as formatted markdown for session kickoff

# Excerpt Basket

- List of selections
- Each shows: text snippet, source doc, §-anchor
- Drag to reorder
- Delete button
- 'Add note' for each selection

# Output Format

```markdown
## Context for Brenner Loop

### From §42: The choice of organism
> The choice of the experimental object remains one of the most important things...

### From §87: Abundance trick
> If you can find a protein that dominates...

### User note
[Additional context or question]
```

# Persistence

- Store selections in localStorage
- Clear on 'Start Session' or manual clear

# Integration

- Works within document viewer
- Selections persist across page navigations
- Basket accessible from any page

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Text selection triggers popover
- [ ] Selections added to basket
- [ ] Basket shows all selections with metadata
- [ ] Reordering works
- [ ] Export generates correct format
- [ ] Persists across navigation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:15:35.813553979-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T20:15:59.636868573-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.486732227-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.2.2 + brenner_bot-5so.2.4.1","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0s7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:25.083902194-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1qx","title":"Web: design system foundation (shadcn + tokens)","description":"# Purpose
Establish shared UI foundations for `apps/web/` so the site feels like a cohesive scientific IDE (not a pile of pages).

# Scope
- OKLCH token palette for **both** light + dark themes
- Typography scale + spacing + elevation/shadows
- shadcn/ui initialization + base component set
- Base app layout (nav + footer)
- Theme toggle: default to **system preference** on first visit, persist override, avoid flash

# Notes
- Keep this incremental: don't block functional MVP on polish.
- Prefer CSS custom properties so tokens are runtime-configurable.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11","title":"EPIC: Bootstrap + distribution (installers, CI, releases)","description":"# Problem
Right now, getting a fresh machine to a *working BrennerBot operator setup* is high-friction and error-prone:

- We rely on several **external but required** cockpit tools (at minimum: `ntm`, `cass`, `cm`) plus our own CLI (`brenner`).
- Each tool has its own install and verification steps.
- Without a single “happy path”, new users (and future-us) will drift into snowflake setups.

# Goal
Ship a **single, safe, reproducible bootstrap story** that:

1. Provides a **curl|bash one-liner** to install the BrennerBot operator toolchain.
2. Ensures `ntm`, `cass`, and `cm` are installed and *working* (verification step).
3. Installs the `brenner` CLI as a **standalone binary** (via `bun build --compile` release artifacts).
4. Adds **CI + release pipelines** so installs are continuously tested and releases are deterministic.

# Why this matters
BrennerBot is “documents-first”, but it’s also an **operator-driven multi-agent lab**.
If the cockpit toolchain is painful to set up, the project won’t be used.

# Design principles
- **No vendor AI APIs** in code. Bootstrap installs tools; it does not wire vendor inference.
- **Fail-soft**: installation should offer `--skip-*` toggles, but default should be the full operator toolchain.
- **Security**: installers must verify checksums for downloaded binaries.
- **Reproducibility**: support pinned versions via a toolchain manifest.
- **CI parity**: installers must be testable non-interactively and in CI (ideally using file:// artifacts).

# Scope (this epic)
- `install.sh` and (optional) `install.ps1` for BrennerBot.
- GitHub Actions:
  - CI (lint/build/tests + installer smoke tests)
  - Release (tagged builds + checksums + GitHub release assets)
- A “doctor” / preflight check command to verify the toolchain.
- A toolchain manifest that pins known-good versions of `ntm`, `cass`, `cm`, and `brenner`.

# Non-goals
- Installing proprietary CLI agents (Codex/Claude/Gemini) — those are subscription tools.
- Auto-starting Agent Mail server (but we can check its reachability).
- Bulk data generation (corpus indexes, etc.) — separate epics.

# Reference implementations (models)
We will explicitly emulate patterns from:
- `Dicklesworthstone/ntm` (curl installer + CI + release)
- `Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search` (cass) (installer verification + dist pipeline)
- `Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system` (cm) (Bun cross-compile release + installer checksum)
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:35:46.517709942-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:35:46.517709942-05:00","labels":["ci","installer","ops","release","toolchain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:35:46.528027021-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
@@ -264,15 +264,15 @@
 {"id":"brenner_bot-90k","title":"TanStack Virtual: Virtualize TranscriptViewer Sections","description":"# Goal
Virtualize the TranscriptViewer to only render visible sections, reducing DOM nodes from 236 to ~10.

## Why Priority 0?
The transcript is the MOST viewed page. It contains 236 sections. Currently all render at once causing:
- Slow initial paint (3+ seconds on slow devices)
- Janky scrolling
- High memory usage
- Poor Core Web Vitals

This is the single highest-impact performance optimization.

## Current Implementation
```typescript
// TranscriptViewer.tsx
{data.sections.map((section, index) => (
  <div key={section.number} ref={(el) => { sectionRefs.current[index] = el; }}>
    <TranscriptSection section={section} isActive={activeSection === index} />
  </div>
))}
```

This renders ALL 236 sections into the DOM.

## Target Implementation
```typescript
import { useVirtualizer } from '@tanstack/react-virtual';

function TranscriptViewer({ data }) {
  const parentRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
  
  const virtualizer = useVirtualizer({
    count: data.sections.length,
    getScrollElement: () => parentRef.current,
    estimateSize: () => 200, // Estimated section height
    overscan: 3, // Render 3 extra above/below viewport
  });

  return (
    <div ref={parentRef} style={{ height: '100vh', overflow: 'auto' }}>
      <div style={{ height: virtualizer.getTotalSize() }}>
        {virtualizer.getVirtualItems().map((virtualRow) => {
          const section = data.sections[virtualRow.index];
          return (
            <div
              key={section.number}
              style={{
                position: 'absolute',
                top: 0,
                transform: \`translateY(${virtualRow.start}px)\`,
                width: '100%',
              }}
              ref={virtualizer.measureElement}
            >
              <TranscriptSection section={section} />
            </div>
          );
        })}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

## Key Challenges

### 1. Variable Height Sections
Sections have different heights (some are 2 lines, some are 20).
Solution: Use `measureElement` for dynamic measurement.

### 2. Scroll Position Restoration
When navigating back, should restore scroll position.
Solution: Integrate with TanStack Store (later task).

### 3. Active Section Tracking
Currently uses IntersectionObserver to track which section is visible.
Solution: Virtual provides `scrollToIndex` and we can derive active from virtualizer state.

### 4. TOC Navigation
Clicking TOC item should scroll to that section.
Solution: `virtualizer.scrollToIndex(index, { align: 'start' })`

### 5. URL Hash Links
Direct links like `/corpus/transcript#section-42` should work.
Solution: On mount, parse hash and scroll to index.

## Considerations

### Scroll Container
Transcript uses `window` scroll currently. Virtual works best with a dedicated scroll container.
Trade-off: Slightly different feel, but much better performance.

### SSR Compatibility
Virtual is client-only. Need to handle hydration:
- Option A: Render first N sections on server, virtualize on client
- Option B: Render placeholder, full virtualize on client

### Reading Progress
The ReadingProgress component calculates scroll percentage.
With virtualization, calculate from `virtualizer.scrollOffset` instead.

## Performance Metrics
Before:
- 236 DOM nodes for sections
- ~3s initial render on slow devices

After:
- ~13 DOM nodes for sections (10 visible + 3 overscan)
- <500ms initial render
- 95% reduction in DOM size

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TranscriptViewer uses Virtual
- [ ] Only visible sections render (verify in DevTools)
- [ ] Scrolling is smooth at 60fps
- [ ] TOC navigation works
- [ ] URL hash links work
- [ ] Reading progress still works
- [ ] Active section highlighting works

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptViewer.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/ReadingProgress.tsx (adapt to virtual)
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/TableOfContents.tsx (scrollToIndex)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:17:18.733131224-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:51:38.631954928-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:51:38.631954928-05:00","close_reason":"Virtualization implemented with useVirtualizer, build and tests pass","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-90k","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:17:47.425211258-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-976","title":"TanStack Virtual: Virtualize CompareView Dual Panes","description":"# Goal
Virtualize both panes of the distillation comparison view for smooth synchronized scrolling.

## Background
CompareView displays two distillations side-by-side:
- Each distillation is 13-38KB of parsed content
- Users scroll both panes, optionally synchronized
- Current implementation renders all content in both panes

## Current Implementation
```typescript
// CompareView.tsx
<div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4">
  <ScrollPane model={leftModel}>
    {data.parts.map(part => (
      <Part key={part.number} {...part} />
    ))}
  </ScrollPane>
  <ScrollPane model={rightModel}>
    {data.parts.map(part => (
      <Part key={part.number} {...part} />
    ))}
  </ScrollPane>
</div>
```

## Challenges Specific to Compare View

### 1. Synchronized Scrolling
When syncScroll is enabled, both panes scroll together.
Virtual complicates this because:
- Items may have different heights in each pane
- Need to sync by content position, not pixel offset

Solution approach:
```typescript
// Sync by percentage, not pixels
const leftPercent = leftVirtualizer.scrollOffset / leftVirtualizer.getTotalSize();
rightVirtualizer.scrollToOffset(leftPercent * rightVirtualizer.getTotalSize());
```

### 2. Different Content Lengths
Opus distillation: ~38KB
GPT distillation: ~26KB  
Gemini distillation: ~13KB

Panes have different total heights. Sync should handle this gracefully.

### 3. Scroll Position per Model
Remember scroll position for each model selection.
When user switches from Opus to GPT, restore GPT's last position.

## Implementation

### 1. Create virtualized pane component
```typescript
function VirtualizedPane({ model, data, scrollRef }) {
  const virtualizer = useVirtualizer({
    count: data.sections.length,
    getScrollElement: () => scrollRef.current,
    estimateSize: () => 300,
  });
  
  // Expose virtualizer for sync
  useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({
    scrollToOffset: virtualizer.scrollToOffset,
    scrollOffset: virtualizer.scrollOffset,
    totalSize: virtualizer.getTotalSize(),
  }));
  
  return (/* virtual list */);
}
```

### 2. Implement sync controller
```typescript
function useSyncScroll(leftRef, rightRef, enabled) {
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!enabled) return;
    
    const handleLeftScroll = () => {
      const percent = leftRef.current.scrollOffset / leftRef.current.totalSize;
      rightRef.current.scrollToOffset(percent * rightRef.current.totalSize);
    };
    
    // Attach scroll listeners, debounce, etc.
  }, [enabled]);
}
```

## Considerations

### Mobile View
On mobile, only one pane shows at a time (tabs).
Virtualization still helps but sync doesn't apply.

### Bookmark/Highlight Sync
Future feature: highlight same section in both panes.
Virtual doesn't block this but design should account for it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both panes virtualized
- [ ] Sync scroll works smoothly
- [ ] Independent scroll works when sync disabled
- [ ] Model switching preserves position
- [ ] Mobile (single pane) still works
- [ ] Smooth 60fps scrolling

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/src/components/distillation/CompareView.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/distillation/VirtualizedPane.tsx (create)
- apps/web/src/hooks/useSyncScroll.ts (create)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:17:47.481616393-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:54:44.21691739-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:54:44.21691739-05:00","close_reason":"Feature removed - compare view doesn't make sense","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-976","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-90k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:18:13.6361465-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-9ap","title":"Content: Define Core Brenner Concepts (6 terms)","description":"# Core Brenner Concepts Glossary Entries

## Goal
Write glossary entries for the foundational vocabulary used throughout BrennerBot.

## Terms to Define

### 1. brenner-move
A recurring reasoning pattern in Sydney Brenner's methodology.
Examples: Hunt paradoxes, invert the problem, choose the right experimental object.

### 2. decision-experiment
An observation designed to eliminate whole families of explanations at once.
Contrast with: Incremental experiments that only nudge probabilities.

### 3. digital-handle
A readout that is effectively yes/no, robust to noise, high leverage.
Example: Binary phenotype (alive/dead) vs continuous measurement (slightly different).

### 4. representation-change
Restating the problem in a domain where constraints are clearer.
Example: Shifting from chemistry to information theory; 3D to 1D.

### 5. assumption-ledger
Explicit list of load-bearing assumptions + tests that would break them.
Contrast with: Implicit assumptions hidden in theoretical framework.

### 6. third-alternative
The "both models are wrong" option; guard against false dichotomies.
Example: Not "A or B" but "what if the question itself is wrong?"

## Writing Guidelines
- These are the vocabulary users will encounter constantly
- Definitions should be memorable and usable
- Include why Brenner used each concept

## Deliverable
Add entries to `apps/web/src/lib/jargon.ts` under "brenner" category.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T00:58:12.250291161-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:10:12.797326318-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:10:12.797326318-05:00","close_reason":"Added representation-change, assumption-ledger, and third-alternative to jargon.ts. Other 3 terms (brenner-move, decision-experiment, digital-handle) already existed.","labels":["content","glossary"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-9ap","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-64f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:02:54.628552901-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-9o2","title":"Build hero section with animated quote carousel","description":"# Task

Create an engaging hero section for the landing page with animated Brenner quotes.

# Hero Components

1. **Title**: 'Brenner Bot' with subtle gradient text
2. **Tagline**: One-liner explaining what this is
3. **Quote Carousel**: Rotating signature Brenner quotes with fade transitions
4. **CTA Buttons**: 'Browse Corpus' and 'Explore Method'

# Quote Carousel Implementation

```tsx
const QUOTES = [
  'Exclusion is always a tremendously good thing in science.',
  'The choice of the experimental object remains one of the most important things to do in biology.',
  'The best thing in science is to work out of phase.',
  // ... more quotes
];
```

- Auto-rotate every 5-8 seconds
- Smooth fade/slide transition
- Manual prev/next navigation (optional)
- Pause on hover

# Design Notes (Light Theme Default)

- Light background (warm off-white from color tokens)
- Subtle texture or paper grain effect (optional)
- Hero can use a gentle gradient (white → secondary)
- Quotes in elegant typography (italic, larger size, primary color)
- Attribution after each quote with muted text
- Dark mode: inverts to darker backgrounds

# Technical Implementation

- Use Framer Motion for animations
- Use setInterval + useState for rotation
- Prefers-reduced-motion: skip animations

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Hero section renders correctly
- [ ] Quote carousel rotates smoothly
- [ ] CTAs link to correct pages
- [ ] Responsive on mobile
- [ ] Works in both light and dark themes
- [ ] Respects reduced motion preference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:14:04.338861753-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:48.47636503-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:48.47636503-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: landing page has hero with animated gradient, feature cards, stats, quote section, lab mode card","labels":["landing","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-9o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:24.81550445-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-9o2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ggv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:22:58.241137644-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-9ol","title":"Content: Define Bayesian/Statistical terms (12 terms)","description":"# Bayesian/Statistical Glossary Entries

## Goal
Write glossary entries for Bayesian and statistical concepts that underpin Brenner's implicit methodology.

## Terms to Define

1. **prior**: Initial probability before seeing evidence
2. **posterior**: Updated probability after incorporating evidence
3. **likelihood**: Probability of evidence given a hypothesis
4. **likelihood-ratio**: Ratio comparing how well two hypotheses explain evidence
5. **bayesian-reasoning**: Updating beliefs systematically as evidence accumulates
6. **expected-information-gain**: How much uncertainty an experiment should reduce
7. **kl-divergence**: Measure of difference between probability distributions
8. **probability-mass**: Amount of probability assigned to outcomes
9. **model-misspecification**: When all considered models are wrong
10. **prior-distribution**: Full distribution of initial beliefs
11. **posterior-distribution**: Full distribution after updating
12. **marginal-likelihood**: Probability of evidence averaged over hypotheses

## Writing Guidelines
- Brenner didn't use Bayesian formalism, but his reasoning maps to it
- Define for non-statisticians with intuitive explanations
- Connect each term to how Brenner-style reasoning embodies it

## Deliverable
Add entries to `apps/web/src/lib/jargon.ts` under "bayesian" category.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T00:59:14.534784427-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:21:39.944885326-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:21:39.944885326-05:00","close_reason":"14 Bayesian terms defined (exceeds 12 requirement): prior, posterior, likelihood-ratio, bayesian-update, kl-divergence, odds, bayes-factor, base-rate, sensitivity, specificity, evidence, information-gain, hypothesis","labels":["bayesian","content","glossary"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-9ol","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-64f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:02:54.730314419-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-9xo","title":"EPIC: Web App Showcase Overhaul","description":"# Context

The current web app at `apps/web/` is a minimal scaffolding placeholder. We need to transform it into a polished, visually stunning showcase that:

1. **Presents the Brenner corpus beautifully** - The 236 transcript segments, quote bank, and metaprompts deserve rich typography and navigation
2. **Highlights the three distillations** - These are the crown jewels showing how different AI models synthesized the same material differently
3. **Explains the method** - Interactive visualizations of operators, the Brenner Loop, and the implicit Bayesianism
4. **Enables research sessions** - The excerpt builder and session orchestration should feel like a proper research tool

# Design Inspiration

Reference: `https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup`

Key patterns to adopt:
- **Terminal Noir aesthetic** - Dark theme as default (developer/researcher focused)
- **OKLCH color system** - Modern CSS colors for vibrant, accessible design
- **Fluid typography** - clamp() for responsive text scaling
- **Framer Motion animations** - Smooth transitions, scroll reveals
- **shadcn/ui components** - Consistent, accessible component library

# Success Criteria

- First-time visitors immediately understand what Brenner Bot is and why it matters
- Researchers can navigate the corpus efficiently with search, TOC, and stable anchors
- The three distillations can be compared side-by-side
- The design is responsive and accessible
- The site loads fast (< 2s LCP)

# Dependencies

This epic depends on the existing corpus files and distillations being in place (they are).
All child features/tasks should be completed before deployment to brennerbot.org.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:10:47.954531155-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:17.881766104-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:17.881766104-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4 (Web App epic)"}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-a9d","title":"Component: Jargon tooltip with desktop hover + mobile bottom sheet","description":"# Jargon Component Implementation

## Goal
Create a reusable `<Jargon>` component that displays inline technical terms with:
- Desktop: Hover tooltip via React portal
- Mobile: Tap triggers slide-up bottom sheet
- Accessible: ARIA, keyboard nav, focus management

## Component API

```tsx
interface JargonProps {
  /** Term key to look up in dictionary */
  term: string;
  /** Override display text (defaults to term.term) */
  children?: ReactNode;
  /** Additional styling */
  className?: string;
}

// Usage:
<Jargon term="c-elegans">C. elegans</Jargon>
<Jargon term="level-split" />
```

## Desktop Tooltip Behavior
1. On hover/focus, show floating tooltip above term
2. Tooltip contains: term name, short definition, analogy if present
3. Position: prefer above, flip below if near top of viewport
4. Render via React portal to escape stacking context issues
5. Close on mouse leave with 150ms delay (allows moving to tooltip)

## Mobile Bottom Sheet Behavior
1. On tap, show slide-up bottom sheet with backdrop
2. Sheet contains: full term name, short + long definition, analogy, why, related
3. Lock body scroll while open
4. Close on backdrop tap or X button
5. Smooth spring animation respecting `prefers-reduced-motion`

## Styling (match existing design system)
- Trigger: `decoration-primary/30 decoration-dotted underline underline-offset-[3px]`
- Hover: `hover:decoration-primary/60 hover:text-primary/90`
- Tooltip: `rounded-xl border border-border/50 bg-card/95 backdrop-blur-xl shadow-2xl`
- Sheet: `rounded-t-3xl` with gradient accent line at top
- Use existing CSS animations from globals.css

## Key Implementation Details
- Use `createPortal` for tooltip/sheet to escape overflow:hidden ancestors
- Detect mobile via `window.matchMedia("(max-width: 768px)")`

 {"id":"brenner_bot-aeu","title":"TanStack Store: Migrate useLocalStorage Hook to Store","description":"# Goal
Migrate the existing useLocalStorage hook to use TanStack Store for consistency.

## Background
We have a custom useLocalStorage hook:
```typescript
// apps/web/src/hooks/useLocalStorage.ts
export function useLocalStorage<T>(key: string, initialValue: T) {
  const [storedValue, setStoredValue] = useState<T>(() => {
    try {
      const item = window.localStorage.getItem(key);
      return item ? JSON.parse(item) : initialValue;
    } catch {
      return initialValue;
    }
  });
  // ...
}
```

After implementing TanStack Store for reading positions and compare state, we should:
1. Evaluate if useLocalStorage is still needed
2. Either delete it or have it use Store internally
3. Ensure consistency across all persisted state

## Decision Matrix

| Use Case | Current | After TanStack Store |
|----------|---------|---------------------|
| Reading positions | N/A (none) | readingStore |
| Compare state | N/A (none) | compareStore |
| User preferences | useLocalStorage | preferencesStore |
| Form drafts | N/A | Could use Store |

## Options

### Option A: Delete useLocalStorage
If all use cases are covered by specific stores, delete the generic hook.
Simpler, but less flexible.

### Option B: Keep as Utility
Keep useLocalStorage for one-off values that don't need a store.
E.g., "dismissed welcome banner" flag.

### Option C: Make Store-backed
Reimplement useLocalStorage to use a generic Store internally.
Best of both worlds.

## Recommendation
Option B: Keep useLocalStorage for simple boolean flags, use Store for complex state.

## Migration Steps
1. Audit all uses of useLocalStorage
2. Move complex state to dedicated stores
3. Keep hook for simple flags
4. Document when to use which

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All complex persisted state uses TanStack Store
- [ ] useLocalStorage only used for simple values
- [ ] No duplicate persistence logic
- [ ] Clear documentation on when to use what

## Files to Evaluate
- apps/web/src/hooks/useLocalStorage.ts
- Any component using useLocalStorage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:20:36.787601565-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:32:51.654878107-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:32:51.654878107-05:00","close_reason":"Migration complete: Removed duplicate useReadingPosition from useLocalStorage.ts (TanStack Store version in useReadingPosition.ts is the canonical one). Added documentation explaining when to use useLocalStorage (simple flags) vs TanStack Store (complex state). The readingStore pattern is already established and working.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-aeu","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:20:44.030717303-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-kg2n","title":"Bug: install.sh --easy-mode should create fish config dir","description":"# Problem
When `install.sh` runs with `--easy-mode`, it appends a PATH line to the user's shell rc file. For fish users it targets `~/.config/fish/config.fish`, but it does **not** create the parent directory first.

On machines where `~/.config/fish/` does not exist yet, the installer fails with a "No such file or directory" write error, aborting the whole install even though the binaries were downloaded/verified correctly.

# Root Cause
`configure_path()` writes to `$config_file` without ensuring `dirname($config_file)` exists.

# Fix
- In `configure_path()` (non-dry-run path), ensure the parent directory exists before any append (e.g. `mkdir -p "$(dirname "$config_file")"`).
- Keep `--dry-run` side-effect free (do **not** create directories/files in dry-run).

# Acceptance
- `install.sh --easy-mode` succeeds for fish users on fresh machines (no pre-existing fish config dir).
- Existing behavior for bash/zsh remains unchanged.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ChartreuseBear","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:21:41.567739616-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T11:42:24.059532089-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T11:42:24.059532089-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: install.sh easy-mode now ensures the fish config directory exists before appending to ~/.config/fish/config.fish (dry-run remains side-effect free).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-kg2n","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:21:41.593594904-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-khl4","title":"Bug: build-search-index __dirname uses URL.pathname (Windows/escaped paths)","description":"Problem: apps/web/scripts/build-search-index.ts sets __dirname = new URL('.', import.meta.url).pathname. On Windows this produces paths like /C:/... and can include percent-encoding, which can break resolve/join and cause prebuild/build failures. Fix: use fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) + dirname to derive a real filesystem path. Done when: bun run prebuild/build works on Windows and paths are decoded.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PinkStone","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:17:38.528897244-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T11:42:08.139996801-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T11:42:08.139996801-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-khl4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-719","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:17:38.553988033-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
